dependency_notes: Strict sequential order: codec(01) -> scheduler(02) -> send(03) -> receive(04) -> watcher(05) -> push-triggers(06) -> §10.1 fix(07) -> e2e/proof(08), matching spec.md §13 and architecture.md 'Implementation constraints'. Each later story consumes only already-landed exports from earlier stories (see top-level `seams`); no story is implemented before a module it depends on. Two same-file cautions: (a) story 04 and story 07 both touch the contactCache.ts write surface — story 04 lands the neutralized-write seam once, story 07 only calls into it (does not re-implement LWW or contact-injection neutralization); (b) story 06 and story 07 both edit app/src/lib/pairing/pairingAck.ts's handlePairingAck admission block — story 06 (announce-on-pair trigger) lands first per story_order, story 07 (importCard name-drop fix) edits the same function afterward and must rebase on story 06's change rather than reverting it. Because implementation is strictly sequential (see base:story-planner 'Module Ownership... not a concurrency license'), no story requires a temporary mock of a later story's module — mock_dependencies/introduces_mocks are empty on every story.
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks are introduced by this epic. Every cross-story dependency points backward in story_order (a later story consumes an earlier, already-implemented module's real export), so no story needs a placeholder for work that hasn't landed yet.

destination.artifact: send.ts#sendProfileRequest consumes the kind constant + encoder

invariants: DM_PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND (21061) MUST be distinct from every sentinel in AC-STRUCT-1's mandatory pre-land list, asserted by a module-load throw
encodeProfileRequest MUST NOT require a local nickname/avatar (AC-PROF-12: requesting needs no local name)

seams.data: profile-request wire payload + kind constant (receive side)

destination.artifact: receive.ts's request-gate dispatch arm consumes the kind constant + parser

invariants: parseProfileRequest MUST be the only accepted decoder for this kind; receive.ts MUST NOT hand-roll JSON.parse on the rumor content

destination.artifact: send.ts#sendProfileAnnounce consumes the kind constant + encoder (answer path)

fields.name: avatar
fields.type: { imageUrl: string } — never null; encodeProfileAnnounce MUST refuse to serialize a null/absent avatar (AC-PROF-6a)

invariants: DM_PROFILE_ANNOUNCE_KIND (21062) MUST be distinct from every sentinel in AC-STRUCT-1's list and from PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND=30 / PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND=0 (different transport, AC-STRUCT-2)
avatar:null or an absent avatar key MUST be rejected by the encoder before a rumor is ever constructed

seams.data: profile-announce wire payload + kind constant (receive side)

destination.artifact: receive.ts's announce-gate dispatch arm consumes the kind constant + parser

fields.name: avatar
fields.type: { imageUrl: string } | null — null/absent classified malformed by the parser

invariants: parseProfileAnnounce MUST classify avatar:null/absent as malformed and MUST NOT return a value receive.ts can hand to contactCache.ts's write function for that payload (AC-PROF-6a)

seams.data: answer-emission contract (reply to a gate-passing request)

source.artifact: send.ts#sendProfileAnnounce(recipientPubkeyHex, localProfile, keys) — runs ensureAvatar, stamps updatedAt=answer-time, seals+wraps+publishes

destination.artifact: receive.ts's request-gate arm calls send.ts#sendProfileAnnounce only after the §3.3 gate (isAllowedDmSender AND active non-archived) and the §3.4 hasShareableName check both pass

fields.name: avatar
fields.type: { imageUrl: string } — never null (ensureAvatar backfills before this is called)

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.type: string (ISO-8601), stamped at call time, never the profile's last-edit time

invariants: MUST NOT be called at all when hasShareableName(profile) is false (AC-PROF-12: deferred, not sent malformed)
The produced rumor MUST be unsigned and delivered only via sealAndWrap addressed to exactly one recipient pubkey (AC-PROF-8)

seams.data: scheduler pure API + persisted schedule store

source.artifact: scheduler.ts exports computeDue(schedules, nowSec), advance(schedule, nowSec), computeIncompleteSet(contacts, cache), and the few-dm-profile-schedule idb-keyval CRUD

destination.artifact: ProfileHealWatcher.tsx calls computeDue/advance/computeIncompleteSet on mount/online/interval; holds no backoff math of its own

fields.name: nextAttemptAt
fields.type: number (epoch seconds); clamped to now+24h cap on load (backwards clock-jump guard)

invariants: advance() ladder MUST be monotonic: 1h -> 2h -> 4h -> 8h -> 16h -> 24h, capped at 24h thereafter, each fire jittered +/-20%
computeDue MUST NOT surface a schedule whose state is 'answered-incomplete' or 'given-up' unless a D4 non-announce reachability signal has re-armed it to 'active' at the 1h floor
a D4 reset-on-activity MUST NOT fire more than once per contact per 24h period

source.artifact: contactCache.ts: a write function that does NOT call rememberContact and returns whether the LWW write landed with a non-null avatar

destination.artifact: pairingAck.ts's §10.1 fix reuses the same neutralized write / importCard surface; it does not re-implement LWW or contact-injection neutralization

fields.name: landed
fields.type: boolean — true iff the write passed LWW (existing.updatedAt < contact.updatedAt, lexicographic ISO-8601 compare) and the entry now has a non-null avatar

invariants: The function MUST NOT call rememberContact for a sender that is not already a stored, active contact (no contact-injection, AC-PROF-4)
Calling it twice with the same announce payload MUST leave the cache in the same state as calling it once (AC-PROF-10 idempotency)

source.artifact: receive.ts exports handleProfileRequest(rumor, senderHex) and handleProfileAnnounce(rumor, senderHex), each gated per §3.3/§3.5

destination.artifact: ProfileHealWatcher.tsx's own ndk.subscribe({kinds:[GIFT_WRAP_KIND], '#p':[ownPubkeyHex]}) strictly unwraps via unwrapAndOpen and routes to these two arms by inner kind

invariants: Both arms MUST be reached only via directMessages.ts#unwrapAndOpen, never welcomeSubscription.ts#unwrapGiftWrap (AC-PROF-5)
Landing the watcher's subscription MUST NOT modify welcomeSubscription.ts or either pre-existing kind-1059 consumer's subscription filter (AC-WATCH-2)

stories.name: dm-profile-codec
stories.title: DM profile-request/profile-announce codec + kind constants
stories.description: New pure module app/src/lib/dmProfile/kinds.ts defining DM_PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND=21061 and DM_PROFILE_ANNOUNCE_KIND=21062, a module-load sentinel-collision assertion (mirroring pairingAck.ts's PAIRING_ACK_SENTINEL_KINDS pattern) against the full mandatory pre-land list, and encode/parse/validate functions for both message shapes. The announce parser classifies avatar:null or an absent avatar key as malformed and never lets such a payload reach a value the receive path could hand to contactCache.ts's write function.

includes: define DM_PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND / DM_PROFILE_ANNOUNCE_KIND and the module-load throw-on-collision assertion against the full sentinel list (444, 5, 7, 9, 13, 14, 21059, 21060, 20602, 25050-25055, POLL_OPEN_KIND=10, POLL_VOTE_KIND=11, POLL_CLOSE_KIND=12, BACKUP_EVENT_KIND=30078, RELAY_LIST_KIND=30051, PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND=30, PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND=0)
encodeProfileRequest/parseProfileRequest, encodeProfileAnnounce/parseProfileAnnounce
reject avatar:null / absent avatar as malformed in the announce parser and encoder
excludes: no NDK/React/idb import surface (pure module only)
no edits to scheduler.ts, send.ts, receive.ts, or contactCache.ts (those are later stories)

stories.notes: Pure, standalone module with no dependents yet landed. lighter_path is false because files_code (kinds.ts, a .ts file) is non-empty per the lighter_path rule, regardless of AC completeness.

stories.name: dm-profile-scheduler
stories.title: Pure backoff scheduler + persisted schedule store
stories.description: New pure module app/src/lib/dmProfile/scheduler.ts owning the backoff ladder (1h->2h->4h->8h->16h->24h cap, +/-20% jitter), due-check, incomplete-set computation (§3.1 rules including the answered-but-incomplete and malformed-announce carve-outs), clock-clamp on load, the 30-day give-up ceiling, and D4 reset-on-activity re-arm semantics, plus the few-dm-profile-schedule idb-keyval CRUD adapter in the same file (mirrors nonceStore.ts/pendingIntent.ts).

includes: backoff ladder + jitter + due-check + incomplete-set computation, no React/NDK import surface
few-dm-profile-schedule idb-keyval CRUD keyed by pubkeyHex (case-folded lowercase)
clock-clamp of nextAttemptAt to now+24h on load
D4 reset-on-activity (excluding announce receipts, <=1/contact/24h) and the answered-but-incomplete / 30-day give-up terminal states with re-arm on a later D4 signal
excludes: no NDK subscription wiring (that is story 05's watcher)
no calls into contactCache.ts or send.ts

stories.notes: Depends only on story 01's kind constants for type-level clarity (not a runtime import). Testable in full isolation via injected clock, per pure-core convention.

stories.name: dm-profile-send
stories.title: Outbound send path: profile-request + gated profile-announce answer
stories.description: New module app/src/lib/dmProfile/send.ts providing sendProfileRequest and sendProfileAnnounce over sealAndWrap (mirrors sendPairingAck), with NDK/keys/signer passed as explicit params (never a singleton). The answer path runs ensureAvatar on the local profile before replying and stamps updatedAt at serialization time (answer-time, never the profile's last-edit time). Sending a profile-announce is skipped when hasShareableName is false; sending a profile-request never requires a local name.

includes: sendProfileRequest(recipientPubkeyHex, keys, ndk) with no local-name precondition
sendProfileAnnounce(recipientPubkeyHex, localProfile, keys, ndk): ensureAvatar -> stamp updatedAt=now -> sealAndWrap -> publish, unsigned inner rumor
hasShareableName(profile)===false short-circuits sendProfileAnnounce to a no-op (deferred, not an error)
excludes: no inbound gating logic (that is story 04's receive.ts) — this module only constructs and publishes outbound wraps
no idb-keyval schedule access (consume scheduler.ts's exports only if needed, do not duplicate its state)

stories.notes: AC-PROF-8 (privacy: unsigned, single-recipient gift wrap, no publishable kind-0) is owned here because send.ts is the sole outbound-construction chokepoint for both new kinds; later stories (06 push-triggers) reuse this module rather than re-asserting the privacy invariant.

stories.name: dm-profile-receive-and-gates
stories.title: Inbound dispatch: strict-unwrap gates, neutralized cache write, LWW
stories.description: New module app/src/lib/dmProfile/receive.ts with two dispatch arms reached exclusively via directMessages.ts#unwrapAndOpen (never welcomeSubscription.ts#unwrapGiftWrap): a request arm applying the §3.3 disclosure gate (isAllowedDmSender AND active non-archived contact) before calling send.ts#sendProfileAnnounce, and an announce arm applying the §3.5 accept gate before writing. Also edits app/src/lib/contactCache.ts to add the neutralized write primitive (no rememberContact side effect; returns whether the LWW write landed with a non-null avatar), shared with story 07.

includes: request arm: §3.3 gate (isAllowedDmSender AND archivedAt==null), rate-limited to at most one answer per authenticated sender per >=1h cooldown
announce arm: §3.5 gate (same predicate) + malformed-avatar rejection (consumes story 01's parser) + LWW write via the neutralized contactCache.ts primitive + schedule-clear on a completing write
forged-binding unit test: rumor.pubkey != seal.pubkey MUST be dropped before either gate, without mutating any contact's cache entry or schedule
archive check (archivedAt != null) revokes both gates in every direction; unarchive restores them
contactCache.ts: add the neutralized write function (no rememberContact call; returns landed:boolean) alongside the existing writeContactEntry, without changing writeContactEntry's existing callers
excludes: no edits to app/src/lib/pairing/pairingAck.ts (story 07 consumes this seam, does not get its own copy edited here)
no NDK subscription creation (that is story 05's watcher, which calls into these two exported arms)

stories.notes: Owns the shared contactCache.ts cache-write seam (see top-level seams). Security-critical: most ACs and the epic's only forged-primitive unit test land here. Must complete before story 07 (§10.1 fix) and story 05 (watcher), both of which consume its exports.

stories.name: dm-profile-watcher
stories.title: Always-mounted ProfileHealWatcher: sweep lifecycle + dedicated subscription
stories.description: New component app/src/components/ProfileHealWatcher.tsx (5th sibling of PendingPairingIntentWatcher/DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher/IncomingCallWatcher in Layout.tsx) driving the due-check on mount, on the window 'online' event, and on an interval, staggering outbound sends in addition to each fire's jitter when more than N contacts are due. It opens its own dedicated ndk.subscribe({kinds:[GIFT_WRAP_KIND], '#p':[ownPubkeyHex]}) subscription, strictly unwraps via unwrapAndOpen, and routes to receive.ts's two exported dispatch arms — leaving welcomeSubscription.ts and the two pre-existing kind-1059 consumers untouched.

includes: mount / online / interval due-sweep calling scheduler.ts's computeDue/advance/computeIncompleteSet
bulk-sweep stagger (on top of per-fire +/-20% jitter) when more than N contacts are due at once

one new dedicated kind-1059 subscription filtered on {'#p':[ownPubkeyHex]} only (never filter gift-wrap subs by author), routed by inner kind to receive.ts's handleProfileRequest/handleProfileAnnounce
1-line mount of <ProfileHealWatcher /> in Layout.tsx alongside its existing watcher siblings
excludes: no changes to welcomeSubscription.ts's subscription or dispatch
no changes to DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher.tsx's or IncomingCallWatcher.tsx's subscription filters
no backoff math in the component itself — all math is imported from scheduler.ts (story 02)

stories.notes: AC-PROF-7 (self-heal convergence) depends on this story integrating correctly with scheduler.ts + receive.ts, but is verified end-to-end in story 08, not owned here.

stories.name: dm-profile-push-triggers
stories.title: Push triggers: announce-on-change + announce-on-pair
stories.description: Edit app/pages/profile.tsx's broadcastProfile chokepoint (called from the nickname-blur and avatar-select handlers) to additionally fan a profile-announce to every active, non-archived contact (staggered/jittered like the sweep), excluding archived contacts entirely. Edit the scanner-side post-add admission point and the issuer-side handlePairingAck admission point in app/src/lib/pairing/pairingAck.ts to send that one new contact an immediate profile-announce, so its avatar is visible without waiting for the first backoff interval. Both reuse story 03's send.ts and story 04's receive.ts paths — no new kind, no new handler.

includes: broadcastProfile fan-out to every active, non-archived contact on nickname/avatar edit, staggered/jittered, excluding archived contacts
announce-on-pair: scanner-side call after admission, issuer-side call in handlePairingAck after admission — one profile-announce to the single new contact

SCOPE AMENDMENT (lead-authorized after initial implementation flagged the gap): welcomeSubscription.ts#subscribeToWelcomes's existing handlePairingAck(...) call site — pass its own already-in-scope `ndk` through as a third argument, `{ ndk }`, so the issuer-side announce fires end-to-end against a real inbound pairing-ack admission, not just in isolation. Call-site wiring ONLY — no change to the kind-1059 subscription filter, dispatch order, or Welcome/join-request fallthrough.
excludes: does not add importCard(senderHex, decoded.profile) to handlePairingAck — that edit, in the same function, is story 07's §10.1 fix and lands after this story
no changes to the gate logic itself (reuses story 04's receive.ts path unmodified)

no changes to welcomeSubscription.ts's subscription filter ({kinds:[1059],'#p':[pubkeyHex]}), dispatch order, or Welcome/join-request fallthrough — the amendment is limited to the one handlePairingAck(...) call's argument list

stories.notes: Shares app/src/lib/pairing/pairingAck.ts's handlePairingAck admission block with story 07. This story lands first (per story_order); story 07 edits the same function afterward and must not revert this story's added announce-send call. Scope amended mid-implementation to add welcomeSubscription.ts (see scope.includes) — without it, handlePairingAck's new opts.ndk field would have been unit-tested but never actually invoked with a live ndk in the running app, leaving AC-PROF-11b's issuer-side half inert.

stories.name: pairing-ack-name-drop-fix
stories.title: §10.1 fix: issuer persists the scanner's name from the pairing-ack
stories.description: handlePairingAck's admission path currently calls rememberKnownPeers/rememberContact but discards decoded.profile. Add importCard(senderHex, decoded.profile) at the same admission point so the issuer persists the scanner's submitted name, reusing the neutralized cache-write seam landed in story 04 rather than re-implementing LWW or contact-injection neutralization.

includes: call importCard(senderHex, decoded.profile) at handlePairingAck's admission point, alongside the existing rememberKnownPeers/rememberContact calls
regression test: a pairing-ack round-trip asserts the issuer's contactCache entry for the scanner carries the scanner's submitted name after admission
excludes: no re-implementation of the LWW/neutralized-write logic story 04 already landed in contactCache.ts
no change to story 06's announce-on-pair call in the same function

stories.notes: MUST be ordered after story 04 (shares contactCache.ts's write seam) and after story 06 (shares pairingAck.ts's admission block). Not marked lighter_path despite being a one-line-class edit: it shares the contactCache seam and edits a function two other stories also touch, and lighter_path is false regardless per the files_code rule since pairingAck.ts is a .ts file.

stories.name: dm-profile-e2e-and-proof-tests
stories.title: E2E self-heal anchor, stranger gates, no-chat/bell proof tests, i18n baseline, e2e tally bump
stories.description: Relay-bucket e2e specs (dm-profile-self-heal.spec.ts and related dm-* specs) publishing only through the app via a second browser context, driven by a new seedDueProfileSchedule(page, {pubkeyHex, nextAttemptAt, attempts, state}) idb-keyval test hook (mirrors helpers/pairing.ts#seedPendingIntent) rather than any NEXT_PUBLIC_* build-time timing override. Adds the foreign-kind proof test closing contactChat.test.ts's pre-existing gap for both new kinds, extends directMessageNotifications.test.ts's existing proof test, confirms the AC-PROF-14 i18n baseline (no new UI copy expected), and bumps the 44/56 e2e tally in CLAUDE.md and the Makefile's inline comments.

includes: seedDueProfileSchedule(page, {pubkeyHex, nextAttemptAt, attempts, state}) test hook writing directly into the few-dm-profile-schedule idb-keyval store; no NEXT_PUBLIC_* backoff override
self-heal anchor: two mutual contacts paired via the real card flow, both avatar-absent, converge to complete {name, avatar} via the loop alone
stranger request/announce gates driven through the real app via a second non-contact browser context
contactChat.test.ts: new foreign-kind proof test for both new kinds (no source change to ContactChat.tsx)
directMessageNotifications.test.ts: extend the existing proof test for both new kinds (no source change to directMessageNotifications.ts)
confirm AC-PROF-14: no new user-facing string introduced by stories 01-07; if any implementation added copy, verify both en/de entries exist in i18n.ts before closing this story
bump the e2e tally (44->N relay, 56->N+12 total) in CLAUDE.md's E2E gate section and the Makefile's inline comments
excludes: no source edits to ContactChat.tsx or directMessageNotifications.ts (AC-PROF-9 is tests-only per architecture.md)
new specs must be named dm-*.spec.ts (not dm-self-heal*, which already exists for an unrelated feature) to land in the relay bucket

test_fixtures.description: seedDueProfileSchedule(page, {pubkeyHex, nextAttemptAt, attempts, state}) — writes a due-now entry directly into the few-dm-profile-schedule idb-keyval store so relay-bucket e2e specs can drive the self-heal loop without waiting on real elapsed backoff time.
test_fixtures.interface: Playwright test helper, analogous to helpers/pairing.ts#seedPendingIntent
test_fixtures.reason: Test-only timing control; must not ship as a NEXT_PUBLIC_* build-time constant in the production bundle (Resolved Decisions: validator gap resolution).

stories.notes: Depends on all seven prior stories being fully landed. AC-PROF-7 is verified here (end-to-end anchor) rather than in story 05, which only integrates the pieces this story exercises.
